Empires & Puzzles Quenell Review: The Wolf Family's Ramping Sniper
Quenell hits fast, then she hits harder. Much harder. Discover if this Wolf Family sniper still has the bite to match her bark in today's meta, or if she's just howling at the moon.

Quenell is a fast mana Nature (Green) sniper from the Clash of Knights event, belonging to the Wolf Family. Released back in late 2021, she introduced a terrifying mechanic: a special skill that hits harder the longer you wait to fire it a second time. She’s like a fine wine or a grudge—she gets stronger with time. This review focuses on her
, assessing whether her original kit can still compete without the stat-boosting costume.

Quenell sports a rugged, battle-hardened aesthetic typical of the Wolf Family. Clad in practical armor with wolf motifs and sporting war paint, she looks like someone who lives in the woods and eats shield potions for breakfast. The art perfectly captures her "Ranger" vibe—deadly, focused, and slightly wild. Compared to her costume, which leans into a more regal, "Champion" look, the base art feels grittier and more aggressive.
Let's be brutally honest: by modern standards, base Quenell's stats are archaic.
While considered high in 2021, this is now lower than many modern 4-star heroes. Top-tier 5-stars are pushing 1300+ raw attack.
These survival stats are dangerously low for the current meta, where single hits can deal 1000+ damage. Without her costume bonus or Limit Breaks, she is incredibly fragile.
A 320% hit to three targets at Fast speed. Even with outdated stats, a 320% multiplier to three enemies is respectable for a Fast hero.
This is her signature move. After firing, she gains a buff that boosts her
You fire once, wait 5 turns, and unleash a thermonuclear 520% hit to three targets.
In modern play, waiting 5 turns is an eternity. You are more likely to fire her as soon as she charges to secure a kill. However, even a 1-turn wait gives her a 360% hit next time.
The costume increases the base hit to 360% but reduces the ramp cap to +150% (and +30% per turn). The base version actually has a higher
damage ceiling (520% vs 510%), but the costume offers immediate power and self-sustainability (healing).
Fast. She needs 8 tiles to charge. With a Level 29 Mana Troop (or Level 23 + Emblem node), she can charge in 7 tiles (ghosting 3 + match 4).
Damage Potential (With Modern LB2 Stats ~980 Attack):
520% x 980 = ~5096 damage value. This is where she can still hurt modern heroes, provided she survives long enough to ramp up.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Wolf Family bonus gives a chance (35%/50% for 2/3 heroes) to make buffs undispellable. This is

for Quenell because her ramping damage buff is dispellable. If paired with Wolfgang or Ludwig, she becomes much more reliable. Note: The costume update changed the family bonus to apply to 1+ heroes, but base Quenell still relies on the original structure unless updated.
Base Quenell does not have innate passives.
This is a significant disadvantage compared to her costume, which gains the "Extended Status Effect Duration" passive.
"Attack Up" is simple and effective. It helps patch up her outdated attack stat, ensuring her hits still sting.
Upon release, Quenell was a meta-defining offensive hero. The combination of Fast speed and Hit-3 was rare and devastating. She was a staple in War attacks and defenses.
The game has moved on. Her 795 Attack stat is dusty. Modern heroes do what she does (Fast, Hit-3) but with 1300+ Attack stats and added utility like Silence or Mana Steal.

. She is still usable in War attacks if you protect her, simply because a 520% hit to three targets is mechanically broken regardless of stats. However, in top-tier play, she will likely die before casting that second, fully-ramped shot. She shines in "Clean-up" teams or Rush wars where she can fire repeatedly.
Higher theoretical damage ceiling (+200% ramp), Ranger class (Bypass).

have the base version leveled, she is still a functional sniper. The Ranger class on the base version is arguably better offensively (bypassing defensive buffs) than the Monk class.

is the way to go. You bring Quenell to kill things fast. If she's taking hits, she's likely dead anyway. Lean into her strength: heavy burst damage.

The chance to bypass defensive buffs (like Counterattack or Defense Up) is invaluable for a pure damage dealer.
Her damage ramp can be wiped away by any dispeller unless protected by the Family Bonus.
She brings damage and nothing else—no cleanse, dispel, or ailment.
Excellent in stack teams (e.g., 3 Green) to snipe out a side of the defense.
Her ramping buff counts toward the tournament bonus.
You can carry her buff between waves, unleashing a 520% hit on the boss wave immediately.

If you pulled Quenell early, she is a fantastic carry. Focus on keeping her alive with healers. Don't stress too much about the "Max Ramp"—firing her whenever she is ready is usually better than holding her and dying.
Base Quenell is a niche pick for depth in Wars. She is not worth Alpha Aethers unless you have the Costume to boost her stats. If you have the Costume, you might actually prefer the
in some fights (to bypass riposte), using the Costume Bonus for stats.
"She’s a beast if you let her ramp up, but nowadays she dies if a stiff breeze hits her."
"The 520% hit to three is still one of the most satisfying things in the game."
"Ranger class > Monk class for a sniper. I use the base form with costume stats for the bypass."

Quenell is a relic of a slightly simpler time—a time when hitting three people really hard was enough to be a top-tier threat. While her stats have rusted, her "Howling Sword" remains sharp. She’s a high-risk, high-reward blaster who demands protection but pays dividends in pure destruction.
A fun, albeit fragile, cannon. Essential if you lack modern green hitters, but strictly a backup singer without her costume.
A comprehensive review of Quenell, the 5-star Nature hero from the Wolf Family. We analyze her ramping damage mechanics, stats, and viability in the current meta compared to her costume.
